description: Adapt Whale shell execution, hooks, agent instructions, documentation, and release packaging so Windows users can run Whale with PowerShell while Linux/macOS behavior remains unchanged.

userStories.title: Add a shared shell resolver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared shell resolver so exec_shell, background shell tasks, and hooks use consistent shell selection rules on Windows and Unix.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an internal/shell package with a ShellSpec type and resolver logic
Windows resolver looks up pwsh first and then powershell.exe
Windows resolver uses arguments: -NoLogo -NoProfile -NonInteractive -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command <command>
Windows resolver does not fall back to cmd.exe, Git Bash, MSYS Bash, bash, or sh
When Windows has no PowerShell, resolver returns a readable error containing PowerShell is required
Unix resolver returns /bin/sh with arguments []string{"-lc", command}
Resolver has test entry points with injectable GOOS and LookPath so tests do not depend on the real platform
Tests cover Windows pwsh, Windows powershell.exe fallback, Windows no PowerShell, Linux, and Darwin
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Route shell execution through the resolver
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want Whale exec_shell to use PowerShell so pure Windows environments no longer depend on Bash, sh, or cmd.
acceptanceCriteria: Foreground exec_shell in internal/tools/bash.go uses internal/shell.Resolve
When resolver fails, exec_shell returns a shell_unavailable tool error and does not start a process
exec.CommandContext uses spec.Bin and spec.Args without concatenating the command into a shell string
configureShellCommand(cmd), cmd.Dir, stdout and stderr capture, timeout, and exit code behavior keep existing semantics
cwd remains constrained by existing workspace safe path logic and cannot escape the workspace
Returned payload command field continues to preserve the user's original command
Remove the old shellCommand helper, or keep it only as a thin shell.Resolve wrapper with no Windows Bash, sh, or cmd fallback
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Route background shell tasks through the resolver
userStories.description: As a user, I want background shell tasks to use the same cross-platform shell rules as foreground exec_shell so Windows background commands use PowerShell reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: runShellBackground uses the shared shell resolver
When resolver succeeds, the background task uses exec.CommandContext(ctx, spec.Bin, spec.Args...)
When resolver fails, the task status is set to failed
When resolver fails, task stderr contains the error message, exitCode is nil, and no process is started
configureShellCommand(cmd) is preserved
Foreground and background shell tasks have no forked Windows shell selection logic
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Route hooks through the resolver
userStories.description: As a hook configuration user, I want hooks to use PowerShell on Windows so Windows hooks no longer fail because sh -lc is hardcoded.
acceptanceCriteria: defaultHookSpawner in internal/agent/hooks.go uses the shared shell resolver
Hooks no longer hardcode exec.CommandContext(ctx, "sh", "-lc", ...)
When resolver fails, hook returns SpawnErr and blocking hooks block according to existing rules
Hook timeout, working directory, stdin payload, stdout and stderr caps, and exit code 2 blocking rules are preserved
Unix hooks still use /bin/sh -lc
Windows hooks execute with the PowerShell argument array
Add or update hook tests covering Windows and Unix platform branches or resolver behavior for real hook commands
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add runtime environment information to the system prompt
userStories.description: As an agent user, I want the model to know the current OS and shell so it generates PowerShell commands on Windows instead of Bash or Linux commands.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/agent/system_prompt.go adds a runtime environment block to immutable system blocks
Runtime block includes current OS such as windows, linux, or darwin
Runtime block includes current exec_shell display name, with PowerShell on Windows and /bin/sh on Unix
Windows block explicitly requires PowerShell syntax, Windows-compatible paths, and Windows environment variables
Windows block explicitly forbids assuming /tmp, grep -r, bash syntax, POSIX variable assignment, and Linux-only shell behavior
Unix block does not contain PowerShell-only instructions
Add a render helper with injectable goos and shellName so tests do not depend on runtime.GOOS
System prompt tests cover Windows and Linux include/exclude assertions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Make exec_shell tool descriptions runtime-aware
userStories.description: As an agent user, I want the exec_shell tool description to state the current shell so the model prioritizes native PowerShell commands on Windows.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/tools/catalog_shell.go renders exec_shell description through a helper by GOOS
Windows description includes Commands are executed with PowerShell on Windows or equivalent wording
Windows description requires PowerShell syntax, Windows-compatible paths, and Windows environment variables
Windows description explicitly says not to assume /tmp, grep -r, bash syntax, or Linux-only shell behavior
Windows description recommends native commands including Get-ChildItem, Get-Content, Select-String, Test-Path, and Measure-Object
Unix description keeps existing shell semantics and does not include Windows-only constraints
Both descriptions preserve that the default working directory is the workspace root, subdirectories use cwd, and commands should not be prefixed with cd
Tool description tests cover Windows and Linux text
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add PowerShell-native read-only allowlist entries
userStories.description: As a Windows user in ask or plan mode, I want common read-only PowerShell exploration commands to avoid extra approval so the agent can read files, search text, and inspect the environment smoothly.
acceptanceCriteria: shellReadOnlyAllowPrefixes or equivalent implementation is extended in a GOOS-testable way
Windows allowlist includes Get-ChildItem, Get-Location, Write-Output, Get-Content, Select-String, Measure-Object, Test-Path, Resolve-Path, Get-Item, and Get-Command

Windows allowlist includes read-only development commands: git status, git diff, git log, git show, git branch, git remote, git rev-parse, git config --get, go test, go vet, go version, python --version, node --version, npm --version, and npx --version
Matching logic remains case-insensitive
Existing Unix allowlist entries are not removed
Unix still allows existing development tool commands including cargo test, cargo check, cargo clippy, rustc --version, and python3 --version
Windows tests cover Select-String TODO -Path *.go, Get-Content README.md -Head 20, and Get-ChildItem -Recurse
Linux tests cover grep TODO README.md and existing Rust/Python read-only commands
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Rename Bash-specific policy and CLI wording
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want neutral shell wording so Windows support is not misleadingly labeled as bash.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/policy/policy.go renames bashCommandFromInput to shellCommandFromInput or an equivalent neutral helper
Decide uses the renamed neutral helper
AllowPrefixes and DenyPrefixes matching semantics are unchanged
internal/ui/cli/cmd/root.go --allow-prefixes help says Comma-separated shell command prefixes to auto-allow
internal/ui/cli/cmd/root.go --deny-prefixes help says Comma-separated shell command prefixes to deny
rg -n "bash command prefixes|bashCommandFromInput" internal docs finds no remaining matches
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Document Windows shell behavior and hooks
userStories.description: As a Whale configuration user, I want documentation for Windows PowerShell behavior and hook examples so I can write correct cross-platform configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/configuration.md adds or updates shell behavior documentation
Documentation states Linux and macOS shell commands and hooks use /bin/sh -lc
Documentation states Windows shell commands and hooks use PowerShell, preferring pwsh and then powershell.exe
Documentation states Windows does not use cmd.exe, Git Bash, or MSYS Bash by default
Hook examples distinguish Unix and Windows
Unix hook example uses echo 'blocked by policy' >&2; exit 2 or an equivalent Unix shell command
Windows hook example uses Write-Error 'blocked by policy'; exit 2 or an equivalent PowerShell command
Shell tool hook match examples use "match": "exec_shell" and no longer use "match": "bash"
Documentation states Whale does not automatically translate Bash hook commands into PowerShell
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Windows release assets
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want downloadable Windows executable and zip release assets from GitHub Releases so I do not need to build Whale from source.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/release.yml build matrix includes windows/amd64
Workflow builds dist/whale-windows-amd64.exe
Windows build uses GOOS=windows, GOARCH=amd64, and CGO_ENABLED=0
Windows build continues injecting github.com/usewhale/whale/internal/build.Version=${VERSION}
Workflow generates whale-windows-amd64.zip
Zip internal filename is whale.exe
checksums.txt covers the Windows .exe and .zip assets
Release upload pattern covers .exe and .zip without breaking existing darwin/linux tar.gz assets
Local command GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/whale succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Windows installation documentation
userStories.description: As a Windows user, I want README manual installation steps so I can download Whale, put it on PATH, and start it.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md adds Windows installation instructions near quick start
README.en.md adds equivalent English Windows installation instructions
Documentation recommends downloading whale-windows-amd64.zip and extracting whale.exe
Documentation says whale.exe can be placed in a PATH directory such as $env:USERPROFILE\bin
Documentation includes PowerShell command examples for whale --version, whale setup, and whale
Documentation states Whale uses PowerShell for shell commands on Windows
Documentation recommends installing PowerShell 7 (pwsh) and states powershell.exe is tried when pwsh is unavailable
Windows documentation does not ask users to use curl | sh
Documentation states direct whale-windows-amd64.exe downloads must be renamed to whale.exe before placing on PATH or run by the full file name
Documentation notes unsigned exe files may trigger Windows SmartScreen and recommends confirming GitHub Release source and checksum
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify end-to-end behavior and residual assumptions
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want tests and static checks to confirm the Windows PowerShell adaptation is complete before release.
acceptanceCriteria: make test passes
GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/whale passes
rg -n "bash command prefixes|bashCommandFromInput|cmd /c" internal docs finds no inappropriate remaining matches
rg -n "sh -lc" internal docs only finds Unix resolver code, tests, or explicit documentation
Release workflow static check confirms Windows .exe, .zip, checksum, and upload pattern coverage
If no real Windows environment is available, task notes explicitly record that real Windows smoke testing is still needed
If a real Windows environment is available, verify downloaded zip extraction and PATH installation allow whale --version, whale setup, and whale to run
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
